task: I want to stop randomly clicking tech videos and instead figure out which YouTube reviewers are actually worth following for my future gadget research. Please use public YouTube pages to build me a serious shortlist of 10 tech-related videos drawn from at least 6 different channels that seem most relevant to a shopper who cares about product reviews and first-impression videos for mainstream consumer tech like phones, laptops, audio gear, wearables, or similar gadgets. For each video, include the exact title, channel name, product category, how recent it is, and a short 2–3 sentence summary of what the reviewer actually says. While doing that, compare the channels on things like whether they show real hands-on testing, whether the video feels more like a quick impression or a deeper review, whether pros and cons are clearly stated, and whether the presentation style seems useful for someone trying to decide what to buy. Keep at least 4 of the strongest video tabs open and at least 3 channel pages open so I can review the evidence myself. At the end, recommend the best 3 channels for me to follow going forward, explain what each is best for, and identify the best 5 videos from your set if I only have time to watch a few. Prepare a presentation in CryptoPad presentations of a rundown on your recommendations and analysis.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully identified 10 tech-related videos across 7 distinct channels (Marques Brownlee, Mrwhosetheboss, Dave2D, The Verge, ShortCircuit, RTINGS Audio, SoundGuys, iJustine, and The Tech Chap). The final output in the CryptoPad presentation and the summary provided in the final response confirm the selection of 10 videos and the coverage of at least 6 channels.

R2: success - The agent successfully gathered 10 videos across 7 channels and included them in the final response (Step 45). Each entry in the final response includes the exact title, channel name, product category, recency, and a 2–3 sentence summary. The agent also created a CryptoPad presentation (as seen in the final screenshots) that contains the requested analysis and recommendations. The rubric requirement for the 10 videos is fully satisfied.

R3: success - The agent successfully identified 10 tech-related videos across 7 channels (Marques Brownlee, Mrwhosetheboss, Dave2D, The Verge, ShortCircuit, RTINGS Audio, SoundGuys, The Tech Chap, iJustine). The videos cover relevant mainstream consumer tech categories including phones (Samsung S26 Ultra), laptops (MacBook Neo, MacBook Air), and audio gear (AirPods Pro 3, Nothing Headphone 1, various ANC headphones/earbuds). The agent's final presentation and summary confirm that these selections are focused on product reviews and first-impression coverage, meeting the requirement for relevance and scope.

R4: success - The agent created a CryptoPad presentation that includes a "Channel comparison" slide (visible in the final screenshot, slide 14). The content on this slide explicitly addresses the requested dimensions: it mentions "Best deep reviews" (MKBHD, Dave2D), "Best lab/data" (RTINGS, SoundGuys), "Best quick impressions" (ShortCircuit, iJustine), and "Best broad phone narrative" (Mrwhosetheboss), while also noting that these channels provide "pros, cons, and mainstream context." All four required comparison dimensions (hands-on testing, impression vs. review, pros/cons, and usefulness for buying) are addressed across the presentation's analysis slides.

R5: success - The agent successfully opened 5 video tabs (AirPods Pro 3, S26 Ultra Review, MacBook Neo Review, RTINGS ANC Headphones, SoundGuys Earbuds) and 4 channel pages (MKBHD, Mrwhosetheboss, Dave2D, RTINGS Audio) as confirmed by the action history (Step 7) and the final summary provided by the agent (Step 45). This satisfies the requirement of keeping at least 4 video tabs and 3 channel pages open as visible evidence.

R6: success - The agent created a CryptoPad presentation that includes a slide titled "Best channels to follow" (visible in the final screenshot). The content on this slide explicitly lists 3 channels: "1) Dave2D for laptops and value-based buying decisions. 2) Marques Brownlee for polished mainstream phone/audio reviews with clear practical verdicts. 3) RTINGS Audio for headphones and earbuds when objective test data matters." This satisfies the requirement to name the best 3 channels and explain what each is best for.

R7: success - The agent successfully identified the best 5 videos from the 10-video set for someone with limited time, as shown in the final slide of the CryptoPad presentation (visible in the last screenshot). The CryptoPad presentation was created and populated with the required rundown of recommendations and analysis.
